_comment_: Pinned sha256 digests for docker images used by APEX benchmark runs. Resolution order is registry -> docker inspect -> bare tag. Add tags here to make published headline numbers reproducible. Format: <full-tag-string>: <sha256:...>. Use the literal string 'unpinned' (or the empty string) to record 'tag known to APEX, digest not yet pinned' so docker_pinning logs a clearer warning. Per BENCHMARK_FAIRNESS_CHECKLIST.md, every published headline number MUST resolve through this registry.

_owners_.commit0: phase1a-commit0 (python:VER-bookworm runtime containers used by Commit0BenchmarkRunner)

_swtbench_pinning_status_: Phase 1c scaffold; per-instance digests will be populated by the first end-to-end SWT-Bench run that observes a clean pull. Until then SWT-Bench images run unpinned with a docker_pinning warning.
_commit0_pinning_status_: TODO: resolve real digests via 'docker inspect python:3.10-bookworm' / 'docker inspect python:3.12-bookworm' on the build host and replace 'unpinned' with 'sha256:...'. Until then commit0 runtime containers run unpinned with a docker_pinning stderr warning. The 3.11 entry exists for repos that may opt in via overrides.
_testgeneval_pinning_status_: Phase 1b scaffold; per-instance/per-version testbed digests will be populated by the first end-to-end TestGenEval run that observes a clean pull. Until then TestGenEval images run unpinned with a docker_pinning warning. Tags follow the kdjain/swe-bench-<repo>-instance:<instance_id> and kdjain/swe-bench-<repo>-testbed:<version> conventions emitted by apex/evaluation/docker_subprocess_runner._docker_image_for.
